title: Justicia pohliana
intro: Justicia pohliana, also known as Pohl's snoutbear, is a species of flowering plant in the family Acanthaceae, native to tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, offering various health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Justicia pohliana include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Justicia pohliana is commonly prepared as a tea or infusion, and it is also used in the production of extracts and tinctures, which are utilized to treat various health conditions.
taxonomy: Justicia pohliana is classified within the family Acanthaceae, commonly known as the acanthus or thorny flower family. This plant belongs to the genus Justicia, which comprises approximately 630 species of flowering plants. The genus Justicia is further divided into several subgenera, with some sources grouping Justicia pohliana under the subgenus Plumeria. The exact classification of Justicia pohliana may vary depending on the source, but it is generally recognized as a member of the Acanthaceae family. Further research is needed to confirm the specific classification of this plant.
